query: "gas prices" OR "gasoline prices" OR "oil prices" OR petrol

articles.title: Top 10 stocks rated ‘Buy’ this week: Brokerages project 17% to 48% returns
articles.description: Indian equity markets ended a quiet week with mixed stock recommendations from top brokerages like Morgan Stanley, Nomura, and Jefferies, highlighting bullish targets on key stocks amid falling crude oil prices.

articles.title: Saudi Aramco Resumes Operations at Ras Tanura Amid Gulf Tensions
articles.description: Saudi Aramco has resumed crude loadings at its Ras Tanura terminal following a four-month hiatus amid industry optimism for normalization. The restart occurs despite tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. Oil supply is recovering across the Gulf region as producers ramp up exports, potentially impacting global oil prices.

articles.title: Oil prices plunge 10% for the week as Hormuz traffic resumes; Brent settles at $71.99
articles.description: Higher tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and easing supply concerns drove oil to its steepest weekly decline in months. Brent fell 4.34% on Friday, while WTI slipped below $70

articles.title: Tensions Rise in the Strait of Hormuz: US and Iran Clash
articles.description: The U.S. military launched airstrikes against Iran after an Iranian drone hit a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions. Meanwhile, Israel and Lebanon signed a ceasefire with Hezbollah, but enforcement remains unclear. The conflict has affected oil prices and raised concerns about regional stability.

articles.title: Easing gas prices give 'fairly sour' consumer sentiment a lift from all-time low
articles.description: Consumer sentiment rebounded this month from an all-time low in May, according to an update Friday from the University of Michigan.

articles.title: Easing gas prices give 'fairly sour' consumer sentiment a lift from all-time low
articles.description: Consumer sentiment rebounded this month from an all-time low in May, according to an update Friday from the University of Michigan.

articles.title: US strikes Iran military targets after ship attack in Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: U.S. Central Command forces struck Iranian missile and drone storage sites and coastal radar locations in response to a June 25 drone attack on the Singapore-flagged M/V Ever Lovely near the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM stated the attack violated the ceasefire and undermined freedom of navigation, pledging continued support for commercial vessels. The incident escalates tensions in the critical waterway, where the IRGC has mandated specific shipping routes, even as global oil prices reflect the instability.

articles.title: Farmers hold flag march against proposed India
articles.description: Members of the Bharatiya Kisan Union (Ekta-Ugrahan) on Friday took out a flag march at Ranjit Avenue to protest against the proposed India-US free trade agreement, rising prices of petrol, diesel and cooking gas, shortage of urea fertiliser and the increasing cost of living. The march began from Ranjit Avenue and concluded near the Deputy Commissioner’s office.

articles.title: Most of Wall Street rises, but sinking AI stocks send it lower for the week
articles.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Most of the U.S. stock market rose Friday after oil prices eased back to where they were before the war with Iran, but drops for stocks swept up…

articles.title: Outgoing IMF chief economist sees risks, shifting trade ties and continued uncertainty on global outlook
articles.description: Strategic petroleum releases helped avert a sharper rise in oil prices as a result of the war in the Middle East, but the global economy faces ​significant downside risks if a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran doesn't hold, IMF chief economist Pierre-Olivier Gourinchas said on Friday.
